title: Operate A Tipper Station Using The Ready-To-Tip Control
summary: High-level operator procedure for normal tipper station use based on the training segment: a tote arrives from an AGV, the operator uses the station controls including the ready-to-tip button, and the tipper clamps, tips, discharges contents onto the chute, and returns the tote to the AGV. The source also notes a self-contained HMI is available for operation and fault recovery, but does not provide detailed screen navigation.
when_to_use: Use for the normal operator-facing tipper station handling flow described in the training source when a tote arrives from an AGV and the station is being operated using the ready-to-tip control.
not_for: Detailed fault recovery beyond the source statement that the HMI supports fault recovery
Any button-by-button HMI navigation not shown in this source segment
Any inferred interlock, reset, or alternate control sequence not explicitly identified in the source

responsibilities: Use the operator-facing controls identified in the source
Use the ready-to-tip button for tipping readiness
Observe the tote through the normal clamp-tip-return sequence

safety_notes: Operators are described as equipped with an E-stop and ready-to-tip buttons.
Use only the operator controls explicitly identified in the source.
Do not infer additional button sequences, interlocks, or HMI navigation that are not provided in this segment.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the tipper, AGV tote position, and chute

access_required: Operator access to the tipper station
system_preconditions: A tote arrives from the AGV at the tipper station
The operator is at the station with visibility of the tipper and chute

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the tipper station slide with station layout, handling sequence, HMI reference, and operator controls including ready-to-tip.

steps.title: Identify the operator station and tipper area
steps.purpose: Orient the operator to the correct station and confirm the source-described tipper arrangement.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station and identify the tipper area. Note that the source states each operator station has two tippers.
steps.expected_result: The operator is positioned at the correct station and recognizes the tipper area and two-tipper arrangement.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station and tipper area are identified and match the source description that each station has two tippers.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot identify the correct station or the tipper arrangement does not match the source-described setup.

actions.label: Identify station
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station and identify the tipper area, noting that each station has two tippers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training slide showing the operator station tipper area and the statement that each station has two tippers.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station cannot be positively identified from the source-supported visual cues
The observed setup does not align with the source description of two tippers per station

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.

steps.title: Confirm tote arrival from the AGV
steps.purpose: Verify the normal sequence has started with a tote presented to the tipper station by an AGV.
steps.instruction: Confirm the tote arrives from the AGV at the tipper station before proceeding with the normal tipping flow.
steps.expected_result: A tote is present at the tipper station from the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote arrives from the AGV and is positioned for the normal tipper handling sequence.
steps.failure_condition: No tote arrives from the AGV or the tote is not presented for the normal tipper sequence.

actions.label: Verify AGV tote arrival
actions.exact_action: Confirm the tote arrives from the AGV at the tipper station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide and transcript-supported handling flow showing tote interaction between the AGV and tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote does not arrive from the AGV as expected for the normal sequence

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An HB will come in, the tipper will be clamped, actuators tip the arm to dump contents onto the chute; slide also states the tote is placed back onto AGV.

steps.title: Use the ready-to-tip operator control
steps.purpose: Use the operator-facing control identified by the source to indicate tipping readiness.
steps.instruction: Use the operator station controls provided for tipping, including the ready-to-tip button identified in the source as the operator control for tipping readiness.
steps.expected_result: The operator has used the source-identified ready-to-tip control for the normal tipping process.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator uses the ready-to-tip button and only the controls explicitly identified in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot identify or use the ready-to-tip control, or additional unsupported controls would be required.

actions.label: Use ready-to-tip button
actions.exact_action: Use the operator station controls provided for tipping, including the ready-to-tip button identified in the source as the operator control for tipping readiness.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator control area on the training slide, including the ready-to-tip button and E-stop references if visible.

stop_or_escalate_if: The ready-to-tip button cannot be identified
The normal sequence appears to require additional unsupported button presses or control logic not provided in the source

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Operators equipped with Estop and ready to tip buttons; transcript references a button that says 'I'm ready to tip.'

steps.title: Observe the clamp-tip-return sequence
steps.purpose: Verify the tote follows the normal handling sequence described in the source.
steps.instruction: Observe the documented handling sequence: the tipper clamps the tote from the AGV, actuates the tipping arm to dump contents onto the chute, and places the tote back onto the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The tote is clamped, tipped to discharge contents onto the chute, and returned to the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote follows the full clamp-tip-return sequence as described in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The tote does not clamp, tip, discharge to the chute, or return to the AGV as described.

actions.label: Observe tipper sequence
actions.exact_action: Observe the documented handling sequence: the tipper clamps the tote from the AGV, actuates the tipping arm to dump contents onto the chute, and places the tote back onto the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text describing tote clamping from the AGV, tipping to the chute, and placement back onto the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote does not follow the described clamp-tip-return sequence
The contents do not discharge onto the chute as described
The tote is not placed back onto the AGV

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.

steps.title: Use the self-contained HMI if needed for operation
steps.purpose: Acknowledge the station interface identified by the source for operation, without inventing unsupported navigation details.
steps.instruction: Use the self-contained HMI as the station interface for operation if needed, based on the source statement that the HMI supports operation. Do not infer screen navigation or button sequences not shown in this segment.
steps.expected_result: The operator recognizes the HMI as the source-identified station interface for operation.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI is available as the station interface for operation, consistent with the source description.
steps.failure_condition: Operation would require unsupported HMI details or navigation not provided in the source.

actions.label: Use station HMI if needed
actions.exact_action: Use the self-contained HMI as the station interface for operation if needed, based on the source statement that the HMI supports operation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training slide reference to the self-contained HMI for operation and fault recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: Detailed HMI navigation is required but not provided in this source segment

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Self-contained HMI for operation and fault recovery.

success_criteria: The operator station is identified and used as the source describes.
A tote arrives from the AGV at the tipper station.
The ready-to-tip control is used as the operator-facing tipping readiness control.
The tote is clamped from the AGV, tipped to discharge contents onto the chute, and returned to the AGV.
healthy_conditions: The station matches the source description of two tippers per operator station.
The tote arrives from the AGV for the normal sequence.
The ready-to-tip control is available and used.
The tipper completes the clamp-tip-return sequence.
The self-contained HMI is present as the station interface for operation.
failure_conditions: The operator cannot identify the correct station or tipper arrangement.
The tote does not arrive from the AGV.
The ready-to-tip button cannot be identified or used.
The tote does not follow the described clamp-tip-return sequence.
Detailed HMI interaction is required but not supported by this source segment.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the tote does not follow the described clamp-tip-return sequence, because this segment does not provide detailed recovery steps.
Escalate if operation requires controls, interlocks, or HMI navigation not explicitly identified in the source.
Use only the operator controls explicitly identified in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tipper station overview, two-tipper arrangement, operator controls including ready-to-tip, and self-contained HMI reference.

missing_details: The source does not provide a button-by-button operating sequence.
The source does not provide detailed HMI screens, prompts, or navigation steps.
The source does not define explicit confirmation indicators after pressing ready-to-tip.
The source does not provide recovery actions for abnormal tipper behavior.
The source does not provide timing expectations or cycle duration.
